title: CLI Command Ergonomics Context-Cost Review
goal: Review non-startup CLI commands for defaults and help text that still encourage excessive routine agent reads.
scope: agentic-workspace root CLI commands, agentic-planning command defaults, and agentic-memory command defaults that are likely first-contact or routine operation surfaces.
non_goals: Implement fixes, redesign command contracts, or promote findings into active work during this review pass.

review_mode.review question: Do routine CLI commands default to the smallest useful output, and do help surfaces make expansion paths obvious?
review_mode.default finding cap: 2 findings by mode default; exceeded to capture one root-workspace, one lifecycle, and one module-specific issue class from measured evidence.
review_mode.inputs inspected first: AGENTS.md, planning state, CLI command help, command contracts, and measured default JSON output sizes.

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-workspace start --profile tiny --task ... --format json; command --help for selected CLIs; default JSON size measurements for workspace, planning, and memory commands
review_method.evidence sources: Measured UTF-8 output byte counts plus command contract defaults and parser help text.

references.label: workspace CLI command contract
references.role: declares root command options and defaults
references.locator: commands: modules, defaults, proof, config, report

findings.title: Several workspace root commands still default to broad payloads
findings.summary: The recent tiny-default work fixed start, implement, and summary, but other root commands still return large JSON by default or lack a profile selector entirely.
findings.evidence: Measured defaults: defaults 183588 bytes, config 80129 bytes, preflight 44862 bytes, modules 32260 bytes, status 53374 bytes, doctor 197934 bytes. The contract still declares proof --profile default full and config --profile default full; modules and defaults expose no profile option.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents following documented command names may spend large token budgets before they have enough context to know which subsection they need, weakening the minimum-default principle.
findings.suggested action: Normalize root command defaults around tiny/router payloads. Add obvious expansion affordances such as --profile compact/full or --section, and make existing default profile help explicit.
findings.promotion target: roadmap or GitHub issue for root CLI context-cost hardening
findings.promotion trigger: Promote if maintainers want to continue the minimum-default CLI lane beyond summary/start/implement.

findings.title: Preflight is described as compact but bundles full takeover context without a profile
findings.summary: preflight is the documented takeover/recovery route, but its default JSON payload is about 45 KB and the command only offers --active-only, not a tiny takeover router.
findings.evidence: uv run agentic-workspace preflight --format json measured 44862 bytes. The help says 'Get compact takeover-safe context' and exposes --active-only, while _run_preflight_command includes startup guidance, resolved config slices, branch posture, local memory, active planning state, obligations, operating posture, and durable intent in one default response.
findings.risk if unchanged: Recovery guidance remains expensive exactly when agents are already trying to re-orient, and 'compact' becomes an unreliable word in command help.
findings.suggested action: Give preflight a tiny default or --profile tiny/compact/full. The default should return token, risk posture, active-state presence, and detail commands; full takeover bundles should be opt-in.
findings.promotion target: roadmap or GitHub issue for preflight profile split
findings.promotion trigger: Promote with root CLI context-cost hardening or when takeover evaluations show over-reading.

findings.title: Module CLIs are uneven: planning summary is tiny by default, but planning report and memory reports remain broad
findings.summary: Planning summary now matches the compact default model, but adjacent planning and memory commands still expose broad report/status/doctor surfaces without profiles.
findings.evidence: Measured defaults: agentic-planning summary 4152 bytes, agentic-planning report 576167 bytes, agentic-memory report --target . 35018 bytes, agentic-memory status --target . 29504 bytes, agentic-memory doctor --target . 55386 bytes, agentic-memory route-report --target . 20789 bytes. Planning report help says 'Report compact planning module state' but exposes no profile option.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents routed from the workspace layer into module CLIs can fall off the compact path and consume very large module reports by default.
findings.suggested action: Apply the same output-profile convention to module report/status/doctor commands, with tiny defaults and full detail opt-in. Treat 'report' as a router unless the caller selects full.
findings.promotion target: roadmap or GitHub issue for module CLI context-cost parity
findings.promotion trigger: Promote after root CLI defaults are aligned, or sooner if module-level docs continue referencing broad report commands.

findings.title: Memory CLI default target detection can fail in nested worktrees
findings.summary: Running memory commands without --target from this checkout raises an ambiguous repository root traceback instead of returning a compact usage error.
findings.evidence: uv run agentic-memory report --format json raised RepoDetectionError: Ambiguous repository root detected (...agentic-workspace, ...ricka). Pass --target explicitly. The exception escapes through the generated command path.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents can encounter noisy tracebacks during ordinary module use, increasing confusion and wasting context even though the remediation is simple.
findings.suggested action: Catch RepoDetectionError in generated memory command handling and surface a one-line argparse-style error with the exact retry command using --target .
findings.promotion target: roadmap or GitHub issue for module CLI error ergonomics
findings.promotion trigger: Promote if memory CLI direct use remains supported for agents, especially in nested or home-directory worktrees.

recommendation.promote: Promote root CLI default-profile parity first: config/proof/defaults/modules/status/doctor/preflight should have tiny/router defaults and explicit expansion paths.
recommendation.defer: Module CLI parity can follow once the root command semantics are settled, unless module CLI docs directly route agents to report/status/doctor.
recommendation.dismiss: No finding suggests removing the full payloads; the issue is default selection and discoverability, not the existence of audit detail.

retention.trigger: After promoted issues or roadmap candidates land, keep only the decision summary and measured-before evidence.
retention.proof surface: Command contract defaults, CLI help snapshots, and measured JSON byte counts before/after remediation.
retention.source retention rule: Store decision, confidence, source refs, and reopen/promote triggers; do not copy issue titles, current status snapshots, long command output, or source summaries unless the copied text is the judgment itself.
retention.distillation path: When decisions are promoted into issues, planning state, Memory, docs, contracts, or checks, keep this artifact as evidence/history and remove or summarize reconstructable source context at the next cleanup.

review_finding.rule: Use only these headings when a prose finding is needed; keep the canonical fields structured.

field_map.Next owner: execution_summary/follow-on routed to or required_continuation/owner surface

handoff_or_closeout.rule: Prefer structured fields; use this shape only for short human-readable summaries.

validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace start --profile tiny --task "Review other CLI commands for ergonomics issues as well" --format json
uv run agentic-workspace <command> --help for config, defaults, preflight, modules, status, doctor
Measured default JSON output sizes for selected workspace, planning, and memory commands
drift_log: 2026-05-07: Review created after measuring CLI default output sizes and help affordances.
